channel.title: NHC Eastern North Pacific
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Eastern North Pacific

item.title: Eastern North Pacific Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
000
ABPZ20 KNHC 222304
TWOEP

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
500 PM PDT Wed Jul 22 2026

For the eastern and central North Pacific east of 180 longitude:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Hurricane
Fausto, located well west-southwest of the southern tip of the Baja
California Peninsula.

Eastern and Central East Pacific:
A tropical wave located a few hundred miles southwest of the coast
of Guatemala continues to produce a large area of disorganized
showers and thunderstorms. Gradual development of this system is
expected, and a tropical depression is likely to form on Friday or
Saturday, while it moves generally west-northwestward at 10 to 15
mph, remaining well offshore of Mexico.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...medium...50 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...high...90 percent.

Well Southwest of Hawaii:
A trough of low pressure located well southwest of the Hawaiian
Islands is producing disorganized showers and thunderstorms.
Environmental conditions appear conducive for some gradual
development of this system, and a tropical depression is likely to
form over the weekend or early next week while the system moves
slowly westward to west-northwestward. The system is forecast to
move into the western Pacific basin on Sunday (Hawaiian Standard
Time).
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...10 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...high...70 percent.

&&
Products issued by the U.S. Joint Typhoon Warning Center can be
found at: www.metoc.navy.mil/jtwc/jtwc.html

Official tropical cyclone forecast information issued by the Japan
Meteorological Agency for the western Pacific basin can be found at:
www.jma.go.jp/jma/jma-eng/jma-center/rsmc-hp-pub-eg/RSMC_HP.htm

$$
Forecaster Hagen
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Hurricane Fausto (EP1/EP062026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...FAUSTO CONTINUES MOVING WEST-NORTHWESTWARD AND HOLDING STEADY IN STRENGTH... As of 5:00 PM HST Wed Jul 22 the center of Fausto was located near 17.5, -123.0 with movement WNW at 8 mph. The minimum central pressure was 982 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 80 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Fausto
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...FAUSTO CONTINUES MOVING WEST-NORTHWESTWARD AND HOLDING STEADY IN STRENGTH...

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Public Advisory Number 17
item.description:
Issued at 500 PM HST Wed Jul 22 2026 
000
WTPZ31 KNHC 230235
TCPEP1
 
BULLETIN
Hurricane Fausto Advisory Number  17
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
500 PM HST Wed Jul 22 2026
 
...FAUSTO CONTINUES MOVING WEST-NORTHWESTWARD AND HOLDING STEADY IN
STRENGTH...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 500 PM HST...0300 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...17.5N 123.0W
ABOUT 925 MI...1490 KM WSW OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...80 MPH...130 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...WNW OR 290 DEGREES AT 8 MPH...13 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...982 MB...29.00 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 500 PM HST (0300 UTC), the center of Hurricane Fausto was located 
near latitude 17.5 North, longitude 123.0 West. Fausto is moving 
toward the west-northwest near 8 mph (13 km/h). A continued 
west-northwestward to westward motion, along with an increase in 
forward speed, is expected over the next several days.
 
Maximum sustained winds are near 80 mph (130 km/h) with higher
gusts. Slight strengthening is possible during the next day or so, 
followed by gradual weakening on Friday and into the weekend.
 
Hurricane-force winds extend outward up to 45 miles (75 km) from the
center and tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 195 miles
(315 km).
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 982 mb (29.00 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
SURF: Swells generated by Fausto are affecting portions of the west
coast of the Baja California Peninsula and are spreading northward
toward the coast of southern California. These swells are likely to
cause life-threatening surf and rip current conditions. Please
consult products from your local weather office.
 
A depiction of rip current risk for the United States can be found
at: hurricanes.gov/graphics_ep1.shtml?ripCurrents
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 1100 PM HST.
 
$$
Forecaster Hagen
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Forecast Advisory Number 17
item.description:
Issued at 0300 UTC THU JUL 23 2026 
000
WTPZ21 KNHC 230235
TCMEP1
 
HURRICANE FAUSTO FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  17
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026
0300 UTC THU JUL 23 2026
 
HURRICANE CENTER LOCATED NEAR 17.5N 123.0W AT 23/0300Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  20 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE WEST-NORTHWEST OR 290 DEGREES AT   7 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE  982 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  70 KT WITH GUSTS TO  85 KT.
64 KT....... 40NE   0SE   0SW   0NW.
50 KT....... 70NE  40SE  20SW  50NW.
34 KT.......140NE 170SE  70SW 120NW.
4 M SEAS....240NE 180SE 150SW 240NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 17.5N 123.0W AT 23/0300Z
AT 23/0000Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 17.4N 122.4W
 
FORECAST VALID 23/1200Z 17.7N 124.7W
MAX WIND  75 KT...GUSTS  90 KT.
64 KT... 35NE   0SE   0SW   0NW.
50 KT... 70NE  40SE  20SW  50NW.
34 KT...150NE 150SE  70SW 120NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/0000Z 18.0N 127.4W
MAX WIND  75 KT...GUSTS  90 KT.
64 KT... 35NE   0SE   0SW  15NW.
50 KT... 70NE  40SE  20SW  50NW.
34 KT...150NE 150SE  80SW 130NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/1200Z 18.3N 130.1W
MAX WIND  75 KT...GUSTS  90 KT.
64 KT... 35NE   0SE   0SW  15NW.
50 KT... 70NE  40SE  20SW  60NW.
34 KT...150NE 140SE  80SW 130NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 25/0000Z 18.6N 132.8W
MAX WIND  70 KT...GUSTS  85 KT.
64 KT... 35NE   0SE   0SW  15NW.
50 KT... 60NE  50SE  20SW  60NW.
34 KT...150NE 130SE  80SW 130NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 25/1200Z 18.9N 135.4W
MAX WIND  65 KT...GUSTS  80 KT.
64 KT... 30NE   0SE   0SW  15NW.
50 KT... 60NE  50SE  20SW  50NW.
34 KT...140NE 120SE  70SW 130NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 26/0000Z 19.2N 137.8W
MAX WIND  65 KT...GUSTS  80 KT.
64 KT... 25NE   0SE   0SW  15NW.
50 KT... 60NE  40SE  20SW  50NW.
34 KT...130NE 120SE  70SW 120NW.
 
EXTENDED OUTLOOK. NOTE...ERRORS FOR TRACK HAVE AVERAGED NEAR 100 NM
ON DAY 4 AND 125 NM ON DAY 5...AND FOR INTENSITY NEAR 15 KT EACH DAY
 
OUTLOOK VALID 27/0000Z 19.9N 142.6W
MAX WIND  60 KT...GUSTS  75 KT.
50 KT... 50NE  30SE   0SW  50NW.
34 KT...120NE 100SE  60SW 110NW.
 
OUTLOOK VALID 28/0000Z 20.6N 147.9W
MAX WIND  55 KT...GUSTS  65 KT.
50 KT... 40NE   0SE   0SW  20NW.
34 KT...110NE  70SE  50SW 100NW.
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 17.5N 123.0W
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 23/0900Z
 
$$
FORECASTER HAGEN
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Forecast Discussion Number 16
item.description:
Issued at 1100 AM HST Wed Jul 22 2026 
000
WTPZ41 KNHC 222033
TCDEP1
 
Hurricane Fausto Discussion Number  16
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
1100 AM HST Wed Jul 22 2026
 
Fausto has not changed much over the past several hours.  Although 
the hurricane is maintaining a partial inner core, intrusions of dry 
air continue to disrupt it from closing off.  That dry air has also 
wrapped into the outer portion of the circulation, causing the 
hurricane to have a ragged appearance.  There is a wide range in the 
latest satellite estimates, but based on a combination of the data, 
the initial intensity is held at 70 kt.  It is possible that this 
estimate is a little generous.  A recent ASCAT-B pass was helpful 
in estimating the 34- and 50-kt wind radii.
 
The hurricane is still moving slowly to the west-northwest at 6 kt. 
The global models suggest that ridging should strengthen and remain 
established north of the system through early next week.  As a 
result, Fausto is expected to move westward to west-northwestward at 
a faster pace during the next several days.  Despite the model 
agreement on the overall steering pattern, there is still a notable 
amount of spread at days 4 and 5.  The NHC track forecast remains on 
the fast and south sides of the guidance envelope, in best agreement 
with the typically most skillful aids, HCCA, GDMI, and TVCN.  This 
forecast is quite similar to the previous one.
 
Fausto has a day or two left to strengthen, but given the dry air in 
the nearby environment, only modest intensification seems likely.  
In a couple of days, progressively cooler waters should end the 
opportunity for strengthening and cause a gradual decay.  Fausto is 
expected to fall below hurricane strength by the end of the weekend 
when it will likely move into a region of westerly shear over the 
central Pacific.  The NHC intensity forecast has been nudged 
downward, and still lies at the high end of the latest model 
guidance.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  22/2100Z 17.2N 122.0W   70 KT  80 MPH
 12H  23/0600Z 17.5N 123.6W   75 KT  85 MPH
 24H  23/1800Z 17.9N 126.1W   75 KT  85 MPH
 36H  24/0600Z 18.2N 128.8W   75 KT  85 MPH
 48H  24/1800Z 18.4N 131.5W   70 KT  80 MPH
 60H  25/0600Z 18.7N 134.2W   70 KT  80 MPH
 72H  25/1800Z 19.0N 136.6W   65 KT  75 MPH
 96H  26/1800Z 19.7N 141.2W   65 KT  75 MPH
120H  27/1800Z 20.4N 146.5W   60 KT  70 MPH
 
$$
Forecaster Cangialosi
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Wind Speed Probabilities Number 17
item.description:
Issued at 0300 UTC THU JUL 23 2026 
000
FOPZ11 KNHC 230236
PWSEP1
                                                                    
HURRICANE FAUSTO WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  17                
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026               
0300 UTC THU JUL 23 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 0300Z THE CENTER OF HURRICANE FAUSTO WAS LOCATED NEAR LATITUDE   
17.5 NORTH...LONGITUDE 123.0 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS NEAR 
70 KTS...80 MPH...130 KM/H.                                         
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        00Z THU AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       00Z THU 12Z THU 00Z FRI 12Z FRI 00Z SAT 00Z SUN 00Z MON
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             12Z THU 00Z FRI 12Z FRI 00Z SAT 00Z SUN 00Z MON 00Z TUE
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
20N 125W       34  6  12(18)   X(18)   X(18)   X(18)   X(18)   X(18)
 
20N 130W       34  X   2( 2)  49(51)   5(56)   X(56)   X(56)   X(56)
20N 130W       50  X   X( X)   6( 6)   2( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)
20N 130W       64  X   X( X)   1( 1)   X( 1)   X( 1)   X( 1)   X( 1)
 
20N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)  13(13)  54(67)   X(67)   X(67)
20N 135W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  24(25)   X(25)   X(25)
20N 135W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   8( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)
 
20N 140W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  19(19)  48(67)   1(68)
20N 140W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   4( 4)  28(32)   1(33)
20N 140W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  11(12)   X(12)
 
20N 145W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  17(17)  31(48)
20N 145W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)  14(17)
20N 145W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   3( 4)
 
20N 150W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  15(15)
20N 150W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)
 
20N 151W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   9( 9)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER HAGEN
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
